chunk_summary: This section outlines role-specific responsibilities for frontline workers in India's nutrition programs, emphasizing clarity in implementation, clinic instructions, and adverse event management for children and adolescents.
learning_objectives: Understand the role-specific responsibilities of AWWs, ASHAs, ANMs, and school staff in nutrition and health programs.
Learn how to write actionable clinic instructions for pediatricians to support frontline workers.
Identify protocols for managing adverse events and exclusions in nutrition programs.
Recognize the importance of role clarity in preventing implementation failures.
key_facts: Anganwadi workers (AWWs) handle supplementary nutrition distribution, growth monitoring, and counseling.
ASHAs are responsible for mobilization, home follow-ups, and linking families to health sessions.
ANMs manage immunization, vitamin A supplementation, and illness referrals during outreach sessions.
Teachers and school health staff administer weekly IFA and biannual deworming doses to school-age children.
Pediatricians must specify when weekly IFA should be paused during therapeutic iron treatment to avoid duplication.
Adverse event drills, such as for National Deworming Day (NDD), are recommended for district staff training.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: For school-age children and adolescents, teachers and school health staff deliver weekly IFA and biannual deworming doses under programme protocols, with the nearest health facility designated for adverse event triage.
document_key_topics: Role mapping for frontline workers in nutrition programs
Adverse event management in health programs
Coordination between health and education sectors
